description_long: This is a large commercial passenger airplane with a wide, cylindrical fuselage and a pair of swept-back low-mounted wings. The aircraft is painted primarily white with blue accents, featuring the 'air Transat' logo prominently on the side and a blue tail fin adorned with a stylized star. Two large turbofan engines are mounted under the wings, and the airplane has a conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The cockpit is located at the forward end of the fuselage, with multiple windows visible. The landing gear is not visible in these images, but the overall design suggests a modern airliner built for medium to long-haul flights.
description: A large white and blue commercial airliner with swept low wings, underwing engines, and 'air Transat' branding.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing the full wingspan, blue tail fin, and the overall streamlined shape of the airplane.
description_view_1: Left side profile highlighting the 'air Transat' logo, engine details, and the blue star on the tail fin.
description_view_2: Bottom-up view displaying the underwing engines, landing gear doors, and the clean underside of the fuselage.
description_view_3: Right side profile similar to view 1, with clear visibility of the right wing, engine, and tail design.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with swept-back wings and no visible winglets; moderate dihedral angle.
attribute_engine_type: Twin turbofan engines mounted under the wings.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Tricycle retractable landing gear (not visible but implied by design).
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed multi-seat cockpit with multiple windows.
